classification.rationale: The context explicitly declares type 'research' and the goal is to investigate algorithms and approaches for iterative BLS transit subtraction — no code is produced.

codebase_snapshot.folder_structure:
src/exohunt/
  __init__.py
  bls.py
  cache.py
  cli.py
  comparison.py
  config.py
  ingest.py
  models.py
  parameters.py
  pipeline.py
  plotting.py
  preprocess.py
  progress.py
  vetting.py
  presets/
    quicklook.toml
    science-default.toml
    deep-search.toml
tests/
  test_smoke.py
  test_config.py
  test_cli.py
  test_analysis_modules.py
  fixtures/
codebase_snapshot.summary: Python 3.10+ exoplanet transit search pipeline using lightkurve and astropy. 14 source files implementing ingest, preprocessing (Savitzky-Golay flatten), single-pass BLS search, vetting (odd/even depth, alias/harmonic checks), parameter estimation, plotting, and CLI. 4 test files using pytest. No existing transit subtraction, residual computation, or iterative search logic.

issues.description: Comparison Matrix for subtraction models could include a 'data continuity preservation' row.
Detection limit finding could include the concrete SNR formula.
Cross-iteration 1% filter threshold needs justification based on grid resolution.

critic_verdict.summary: Comprehensive research covering all 8 topics with code-level analysis and prioritized recommendations. All key questions answered with evidence. Minor structural improvements possible.
